tweets.text:
They can suppress crude prices all they want, but at the end of the day, consumers use products. And in this case, gasoline prices have to go up to price OUT exports. If it doesn't, US won't have the storage buffers it needs.

And since the US will have to push prices up to price out exports, the rest of the world will need more gasoline, so unless China decides to lift export bans and start pumping out 2.5 million b/d of gasoline via exports, you just hit the wall.

The growing divergence between refining margins and crude prices means that, at this point, it doesn't matter what your product yield is; you just buy crude because you are going to make money. We are headed there.
tweets.full_text:
They can suppress crude prices all they want, but at the end of the day, consumers use products. And in this case, gasoline prices have to go up to price OUT exports. If it doesn't, US won't have the storage buffers it needs.

And since the US will have to push prices up to price out exports, the rest of the world will need more gasoline, so unless China decides to lift export bans and start pumping out 2.5 million b/d of gasoline via exports, you just hit the wall.

The growing divergence between refining margins and crude prices means that, at this point, it doesn't matter what your product yield is; you just buy crude because you are going to make money. We are headed there.
